description_long: This object is a rectangular, reddish-brown stone block with a rough, textured surface and a series of evenly spaced rectangular holes on its top face. The block's sides are solid, with the long faces showing a slightly uneven, natural stone finish, while the short faces are more uniform. The top surface features two rows of six rectangular cavities, giving it a perforated appearance typical of construction bricks or blocks. The color is a consistent earthy terracotta, suggesting it is made from fired clay or a similar material. The block appears dense and heavy, with no visible engravings or markings. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended for use in construction or as a modular barrier in urban environments.
description: A reddish-brown, rough-textured stone block with rectangular holes on its top surface, resembling a construction brick. The block is solid and heavy, with a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The long side of the block is visible, showing the rough, textured surface and the full array of rectangular holes on the top.
description_view_1: The short end of the block is shown, highlighting the uniform, solid face and the depth of the rectangular holes on top.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, similar to view 0, with the rough texture and the top holes clearly seen.
description_view_3: The other short end is shown, mirroring view 1, with a solid face and visible top cavities.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears relatively clean with minor textural irregularities.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall or barrier
